questions.question: Explain the differences between the following: (i) int const *p (ii) int * const p (iii) int const * const p. Also explain what is meant by pointer to pointer (int **p) and give a practical use case where a pointer to pointer is essential.
keyPoints: const pointer vs pointer to const vs const pointer to const
int const *p - pointer to constant (value cannot be modified)
int * const p - constant pointer (address cannot be modified)
Pointer to pointer use cases: dynamic 2D arrays, modifying pointers in functions, linked list implementations

questions.question: What are memory leaks and dangling pointers in C? Explain how each occurs during dynamic memory allocation. Provide C code examples demonstrating both scenarios and explain how to prevent them.
keyPoints: Memory leak: allocated memory not freed before losing reference
Dangling pointer: pointer referencing freed memory or invalid address
Prevention: always pair malloc with free, set pointer to NULL after freeing
Common causes: missing free(), returning stack pointer, double free()

questions.question: Write a complete C program that uses function pointers to implement a generic array processing system. The program should: (a) Define a function pointer type for comparison functions, (b) Implement bubble sort using this function pointer to allow sorting in both ascending and descending order, (c) Implement a generic search function using function pointers. Explain how function pointers work and demonstrate the concept of callback functions with practical examples.
keyPoints: Function pointer declaration, initialization, and invocation
Callback mechanism: passing functions as arguments
Bubble sort implementation with function pointer for comparison
Generic search using function pointer for custom comparison logic

questions.question: Explain the fundamental differences between stack and heap memory allocation in C. Write a C program to: (a) Dynamically allocate a 3x4 matrix using malloc, (b) Accept input values and store them in the matrix, (c) Display the matrix, (d) Properly free all allocated memory. Additionally, discuss the relationship between arrays and pointers, how string literals are stored in memory, and explain the concept of volatile pointers with practical examples.
keyPoints: Stack: automatic allocation, fixed size, faster; Heap: manual allocation, variable size, slower
Dynamic 2D array allocation using malloc and proper deallocation
Arrays and pointers relationship: array name as pointer to first element
String storage: string literals in read-only memory, volatile pointer use for hardware registers
